title: The stack and the heap

exercises.title: Stack frame reporter
exercises.prompt_md:
## Goal
Write a program that demonstrates stack frame behavior by reading an integer and passing it through a chain of two functions, printing a message at each level.

## Input
A single integer `n` on stdin.

## Output
Exactly three lines:
```
main received <n>
level1 received <n>
level2 received <n>
```
where `<n>` is the input value.

## Requirements
- Write two functions `level1(int v)` and `level2(int v)`, each printing its line and then calling the next.
- `main` prints its line, then calls `level1`.
- Use `std::cout` for all output. Each line ends with `'\n'`.

test_cases.label: Sample: input 7
test_cases.expected_stdout:
main received 7
level1 received 7
level2 received 7

test_cases.label: Hidden: input 0
test_cases.expected_stdout:
main received 0
level1 received 0
level2 received 0

test_cases.label: Hidden: negative input
test_cases.expected_stdout:
main received -42
level1 received -42
level2 received -42

exercises.title: Heap array statistics
exercises.prompt_md:
## Goal Read `n` integers into a heap-allocated array, compute the sum and the count of positive values, print the results, and properly free the memory. ## Input - Line 1: a positive integer `n` (1 <= n <= 100). - Line 2: `n` space-separated integers. ## Output Exactly two lines: ``` sum: positives:

``` where `` is the sum of all elements and `

` is the count of elements strictly greater than zero. ## Requirements - Allocate the array on the heap with `new int[n]`. - Free the array with `delete[]` before `main` returns. - Use `std::cout` for output. Each line ends with `'\n'`.
